description_long: This is a fragmentary ceramic bowl with a roughly circular base and a high, uneven rim, suggesting it is broken and missing a significant portion of its original structure. The exterior surface features hand-painted horizontal bands in blue and orange, interspersed with a row of blue oval or leaf-like motifs. The ceramic material appears unglazed and has a matte, slightly rough texture, with visible chips and worn areas along the rim and body. The interior is plain, showing the natural color of the fired clay, with some discoloration and surface irregularities. The bowl is hollow and lightweight, with a form typical of utilitarian or domestic vessels found in historical or archaeological urban contexts.
description: A broken, hand-painted ceramic bowl with blue and orange bands and a rough, matte finish. The rim is uneven and chipped, and the bowl shows signs of age and wear.
description_view_0: The bowl is seen from above, highlighting its uneven, broken rim and plain interior surface.
description_view_1: This side view shows the highest remaining section of the rim and reveals the painted blue and orange bands on the exterior.
description_view_2: The back view displays the most complete section of the painted exterior, with clear horizontal stripes and blue motifs.
description_view_3: This angled view emphasizes the curvature of the bowl and the decorative patterns, as well as a large chip on the rim.
attribute_decorative_patterns: Hand-painted horizontal blue and orange bands with blue oval motifs on the exterior.
